title: fiwa sasakii
intro: Fiwa sasakii, also known as the "Mata'iapo" tree, is a plant native to the Cook Islands, specifically Rarotonga, and is a member of the Annonaceae family. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Fiwa sasakii include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat skin conditions such as eczema and wounds. Traditionally, herbal preparations of Fiwa sasakii include making infusions by steeping the bark in water, as well as using the leaves to create topical remedies for skin conditions.
